reasoning: The base rate for an unexpected, massive corporate restructuring at a $300B level taking place within 4 months of the resolution date (August 2025 -> Jan 1 2026) is already very low unless there was an active, announced plan in motion. The key piece of evidence here is that OpenAI *did* have a plan to remove the nonprofit board's control of the commercial entity, which culminated in May 2025. In May 2025, after engagement with the Attorneys General of California and Delaware and public pressure, OpenAI officially abandoned its restructuring plans to separate its for-profit business from the nonprofit. Sam Altman and board chair Bret Taylor formally declared that the nonprofit would remain in control as the commercial operations transition into a Public Benefit Corporation (PBC). Recent evidence from July 2025 confirms that an OpenAI-convened advisory board has doubled down on this structure, urging that the non-profit retain control to ensure safe AI development. Because OpenAI explicitly backed down from the exact scenario described in the question only a few months ago under legal scrutiny, the likelihood that it will abruptly reverse course again, execute a new complex restructuring, and complete it before January 1, 2026, is virtually nonexistent. Therefore, I place the probability at the minimum allowable lower bound.
